post.summary: this method requests the auth server give you a token for a particular "application"
post.description: this method requests the auth server give you a token for a particular "application"

requestBody.description: this method requests the auth server give you a token for a particular "application"

SiteId.description: Id of the application to which you wish to obtain an access token.  The list of available site ids is returned from either @see(FormsAuthentication) or @see(SAMLAuthentication)

post.summary:
Provide a username and SHA1 encoded password to log into the system.  It is always recommended that this method be called via a secured method. 
            Should be followed by a call to @see(ApplicationLogin)

Provide a username and SHA1 encoded password to log into the system.  It is always recommended that this method be called via a secured method. 
            Should be followed by a call to @see(ApplicationLogin)

NewPassword.description: If a password change is required, provide it here.

post.summary: Returns the details for a particular application.  This is the same information as returned when you call @see(ApplicationLogin) but you will not receive an access token from this method.

SiteId.description: Id of the application to which you wish to obtain details.

post.summary: Logs the requested token out of the authentication system and out of any application for which a call has been made to @see(ApplicationLogin)

post.summary: If you have configured NTLM authentication properly, you can use this to get an authentication token.  Please note you must have configured IIS and everything correctly for this method to function.

StatusCode.description: A string constant representing the success or failure of the method call. See the status codes table below.

DebugMessage.description: By default, an empty array []. Internal debug messages can be exposed by changing a configuration setting on the server, but be aware that this can leak internal implementation details such as stack calls or method names to the caller.

Detail.description:
Optionally return additional information about this API call
            This will typically contain the reason WHY a method failed (if it fails), warnings if success, and other bits of data that
            are NOT part of the main result.

From.description: What is the first result that should be returned? Note that this is 0-based (0 is the very first row). If this value is not supplied then 0 is assumed.

To.description:
What is the last result that should be returned? If this value is not supplied then all results are assumed. It is safe to provide a number that is larger than the total number of results.
            available.

Total.description: How many results are available in total? This is not used when passing the cursor to a method; it is populated (read only) when the cursor is returned from any method that uses a cursor.

SortDescending.description: If we want to sort the resulting list, do we want it sorted in descending order?  The default is ascending.

SortProperty.description: Property on the object by which you would like to sort.  If an invalid or null property is provided, default sorting will be applied as defined in each API method.

APICursor.description:
A cursor is an optional set of data that controls how to get a subset of a list of data from the API.
            This parameter can optionally be sent as a parameter to filter the result set returned.
            If a cursor is passed to an API method then a cursor will also be returned from the API with additional information populated so that it's easy to calculate the number of pages and find the correct page number
            NOTE that a cursor will NOT be returned if it was NOT passed in the initial method call, as all matching results will already have been returned at that point.
            This is typically used when paging large sets of data in a UI.
            If a cursor is not passed then all matching results are returned.

x-enum-descriptions: Connecting to Tridion for Profile Rule Export, etc
The Realtime Rules Engine (RERE) can use the Campaigns Auth server - so list it here.

Thumbprint.description:
Thumbprint of the certificate, typically obtained by viewing the properties of the certificate.  The thumbprint property's hex string is placed here.
            For certificates which are used as part of SAML processing, this thumbprint can be a name instead.

System.description: Is this certificate a system certificate?  If you create a system certificate, you will be unable to delete it later.  This value would typically be set to false.

Settings.description: Collection of settings which this issuer uses as assertions for incoming SAML authentication requests

Issuer.description: Issuer of Certificate for single sign on

WindowsAuthentication.description: Does this login use external authentication?

NewPassword.description: If you want to update the password for a login, include a NewPassword.  You can use this in a call to @see(UpdateLogin)

Enabled.description: Is this login flagged as disabled

PasswordExpires.description: If set, this is when the password for this login will expire.  Any changes to this will be compared to the password policy

LastLogin.description: Last time this login was used

Modified.description: Last time this login was modified

SiteId.description:
If this setting comes from or should be applied to Login+Site, this is the SiteId.
            If this setting comes from Source = 1 (site), this setting will be ignored

SettingAppliesTo.description:
How can you use these settings?
            Please note that this it possible to combine these
            For instance, for a setting which is available for both logins and sites, a value of 3 is used
            For SAML Assertions, it would typically be 6 because they are applied at both the issuer and the login 
            Issuer settings don't actually have values associated with the issuer; the issuer settings are the assertions which are applied to the login
            Values 1,2,3 will be what is returned when you populate a @see(LoginInfo)
            Showing where the value for this login came from.
x-enum-descriptions: Applies to logins, regardless of sites

name.description: The name of the method or class that the validation rules apply to

validationRules.description:
A list of name-object pairs. The name is the parameter (of the method) or property name (of the class), and the object is the list of @see(ValidationRule) rules that apply to it
            Each rule has it's own type to determine how it is evaluated, but if they all pass then the data is valid.

APIMetadata.description: A class that contains information about the API itself in a format suitable for parsing by another application

APIMetadataType.description: An enum which can be used to filter down the request to a particular type. If this is not specified then all are returned
x-enum-descriptions: Show only method calls and the rules for the parameters in the method
Show only class types, and the rules for the properties in the class

NumChars.description: Minimum number of non-numeric, non-special characters

NumNumeric.description: Minimum number of numeric values in password

NumSpecial.description: Minimum number of special characters in password (special characters include %[`!@#$%&*()_=+\|{};",/])

RequireMixedCase.description: Are Upper and Lower case characters required

PasswordReuse.description: Can the password be changed to a previously used value

Name.description: Name of the setting, must be unique

IsSystem.description: Is this a system setting?  if so, any attempts to modify it will result in an AccessDenied error.  You can create a setting with IsSystem but, once created, you will not be able to change it [or delete it]

Value.description: This is only filled in with a call to @see(GetSettingsValues).  It is ignored when creating settings

host.description:
This is the name of the website in the IMS installation which is running the webserver.
            The Host value is used to control access to this Tridion site, and by default will allow access to every user in this host.

isHTTPS.description:
This is a flag to say whether the Tridion site is available in an HTTPS installation of IMS. 
            If your IMS installation is using HTTPS then set this to 1, otherwise leave it as 0.

Id.description: Internal identifier for the authentication site

SiteTypeId.description: Type of the authentication site.  See @see(GetSiteTypes) to obtain a list of type ids

AuthSite.description: Provides details about an authentication site

Name.description: Name of the authentication site type

Id.description: Internal identifier for the authentication site type

AuthSiteType.description: Contains information about an authentication site type
